name: MDAnalysis (Molecular Dynamics Trajectory Analysis)
pricing_notes: Open source (GPL-2.0+); MDAnalysis developers / Oliver Beckstein

agent_readiness.notes: Molecular dynamics trajectory analysis. import MDAnalysis as mda. u = mda.Universe('topology.gro', 'trajectory.xtc'). sel = u.select_atoms('protein and backbone'). sel.positions. sel.center_of_mass(). for ts in u.trajectory: .... RMSD: from MDAnalysis.analysis import rms; R = rms.RMSD(sel).run(); R.results.rmsd. RMSF. Contacts. H-bonds. PCA. Waterbridge. Solvent. Native contacts. 40+ formats: GROMACS, AMBER, CHARMM, NAMD, OpenMM, LAMMPS. AtomGroup selection language.

gotchas: Domain-specific — only for molecular dynamics trajectory analysis
Large trajectories need memory-efficient iteration (don't load everything)
AtomGroup selection language is powerful but has quirks with complex selections
best_when: RMSD, RMSF, contact analysis of protein simulations
H-bond, solvent, and native contact analysis
Custom trajectory analysis with AtomGroup selection
avoid_when: Structure preparation and system building (use AmberTools or CHARMM-GUI)
summary: MDAnalysis provides MD trajectory analysis for all major simulation codes. mda.Universe('topology.gro', 'trajectory.xtc'). select_atoms('protein and backbone'). AtomGroup.positions. RMSD, RMSF, H-bonds, contacts. Iterate over trajectory frames. 40+ formats (GROMACS, AMBER, CHARMM, NAMD). GPL-2.0+ by Oliver Beckstein. Gold standard for MD trajectory analysis. Copyleft license. Efficient frame iteration needed for large trajectories.
